_spec.purpose: Dedupe ledger for carousel topics. The routine MUST read this before topic selection and MUST append the chosen topic after delivery. No topic may repeat within 90 days unless a material new development exists AND the deck is framed as an update (hard fail otherwise).
_spec.matching: Judge semantic similarity, not string equality: compare candidate against topic + angle + entities + keywords of every entry from the last 120 days. Same underlying story reported by a different outlet or with a new URL is STILL a repeat.

entry_schema.headline: the cover line used
entry_schema.outcome_notes: filled by later runs if the human reports performance

entries.topic: Alaska's data center decision month, anchored by the STAK Energy North Slope AI data center lease revolt (ADL 422741)
entries.angle: The 500-plus vs fewer-than-12 public comment revolt on the STAK lease is one of four Alaska AI-infrastructure decisions in July 2026 (Air Force EUL Jun 29, GVEA turbine Jul 13, AKLNG vote Jul 16, STAK comment Jul 17); thesis 'four rooms, one open door' -- only the DNR comment process invites the public.
entities: Alaska DNR Division of Oil and Gas
entries.headline: 500 wrote in. Fewer than 12 said yes.

entries.topic: AI drone plus computer-vision salmon counting pilot on the Wood River, tested against Bristol Bay's 70-year human counting towers (2026 sockeye season)
entries.angle: An unproven AI counter still in training is tested against the hand-clicker tower method that has set Bristol Bay escapement since 1955; thesis 'one river, two ways to count it, only one is proven' -- honest about a pilot, and about who counts (partners UW FRI, BBRSDA, BBEDC; ADF&G not directly involved).
entities: Alaska Department of Fish and Game
University of Washington Fisheries Research Institute
entries.headline: One river. Two ways to count it. Only one is proven.

entries.topic: Alaska-built AI volcano monitoring (VOISS-Net seismic deep learning + VolcSARvatory InSAR) watching three simultaneously elevated Alaska volcanoes, scaling to all five USGS observatories
entries.angle: Present-tense vigilance: Great Sitkin erupting (Watch/Orange) with Kupreanof and Shishaldin restless, watched by machine senses built at UAF/AVO (a neural ear + a satellite eye); the blind-mountain tension (Kupreanof has no ground network) and the Alaska-tech-exported-national pride arc.
entries.headline: Three Alaska volcanoes are awake.

entries.topic: Cook Inlet gas STORAGE crisis: RCA denies Enstar's $240M Kenai Loop Pool storage prudency request (Jul 10) as Southcentral's supply ceiling meets AI data-center gigawatt demand
entries.angle: Supply-side ceiling, not decision access. Alaska is asked to power a gigawatt AI boom (STAK 1+GW, some 30% over urban peak) on a gas system that cannot even secure one winter's storage; the RCA rejected the storage fix on July 10 for 'lacking basic information' and Enstar suspended it. Thesis 'store one winter first?' plus The Hold Test (hold the winter, the ground, the bill).
entries.headline: Alaska's gas machine, in cutaway. And the valve that just shut.

entries.topic: Alaska as the world's proving ground for autonomous wildfire-fighting robots: the XPRIZE Wildfire Autonomous Response track finals field-tested via UAF ACUASI on a 1,000 sq km Alaska range
entries.angle: Alaska is the arena, not the backdrop; five finalists (Anduril, Data Blanket, Dryad, FireSwarm Solutions, Wildfire Quest) race to close the detect-decide-dispatch-douse loop with no human, decided September 2026; thesis 'the machines are still auditioning' (a contest winner is not proof), told through a closing Autonomy Ring
entries.headline: First Machine to the Fire

entries.topic: GVEA's July 13 board vote on a second ~$120M LM6000 gas turbine, set against the Air Force AI-data-center land leases and a record fuel-charge spike
entries.angle: Firm power, soft load: a member-owned Interior co-op is asked to finance firm, dated gas capacity now (member debt, ratepayer exposure) while the AI loads that make firm power feel urgent (Air Force EULs at Eielson/Clear/JBER) are still proposals; who pays for the megawatts, decided in executive session. Distinct from No.1's civic-access 'four rooms' frame (this is Interior generation adequacy / who-pays) and from No.4's Cook Inlet gas storage.
entries.headline: The Interior's Power Math ($120 million, one vote today, members own the bill)

entries.topic: ReconCraft (Anchorage, Native-owned SBA 8(a)) won a $24.96M sole-source Navy contract for autonomous low-profile (stealth) vessels, built in Clackamas, Oregon
entries.angle: What 'Alaska' means in the autonomous-defense build-out is, here, a contracting instrument (Native-owned 8(a) status plus an Anchorage address) rather than a shipyard; the boats are welded Outside. Thesis 'the boat is Alaskan, the shipyard is not' via a single waterline that splits Alaska's paper footprint from its physical one.
entries.headline: The boat is Alaskan. The shipyard is not.

entries.topic: NSF Critical Mineral Accelerator Engine in Alaska (UAF-led, announced 2026-07-14): a Regional Innovation Engine funded $15M now and up to $160M over 10 years if milestones met, to use AI to help locate mineral deposits and biological methods to extract them
entries.angle: Honesty-as-edge. Alaska won a real, decade-scale federal bet to find and process critical minerals, but the AI headlining it is a single unbuilt sentence (no model, dataset, or site named) and the $160M is a milestone-gated ceiling; thesis the uncomputed ground / it isn't built yet, the money framed as a ladder not a check, NANA a partner not owner, one illustrative named site.
entries.headline: It isn't built yet.

entries.topic: Quinhagak / Nalaquq Yup'ik drone-piloting and machine-vision training pipeline in the roadless Yukon-Kuskokwim Delta (Native-owned Qanirtuuq subsidiary; a search-and-rescue boat-detection CNN and a polarized-lens salmon counter)
entries.angle: Data-sovereignty of the sensing layer. A village corporation trains its own FAA Part 107 pilots AND owns its own machine vision, so the community owns the machine that watches its own land, waters, and people. Honesty firewall: the training is HUMAN-piloted (never autonomous AI); the only real ML is two Nalaquq prior tools (SAR CNN c14, polarized-lens salmon counting c15) cited as prior published work; thermal reindeer counting and archaeology are human-read, NOT AI. Thesis 'own the eye, don't rent it'. Distinct from No.2 (Wood River AI salmon counting) by subject/place/actor and from No.1 (STAK who-decides) entirely.
entries.headline: No Road Out. Quinhagak Flies Its Own Eyes
entries.sources_lead: KYUK 2026-07-14 (Samantha Watson); Alaska Public Media / Alaska News Nightly 2026-07-14; nalaquq.com; JCMC paper (SAR CNN + salmon counting, background)
entries.outcome_notes: Shipped 9.07 vs 8.3, vector PDF 7.86MB, 9 slides, zero hard fails. 6 scouts + fact-checker + 3 treatments + copywriter + 5 pixel critics (8 ship / 1 revise-then-fixed) + flow (SHIP) + scorer, full studio, no usage-limit degradation. S6 AK3D thermal terrain hero resolved the chronic flat-hero weakness. Runner-up = AIDEA Houston 30-sq-mi free-land data-center giveaway (now tracked on the public docket).

entries.topic: Will an Alaska data center raise your power bill: on-grid vs off-grid and the cost-allocation gap
entries.angle: A myth-correcting ratepayer-economics analysis. Off-grid North Slope (STAK) is behind-the-meter and cannot touch Railbelt bills; the on-grid AIDEA Mat-Su park just advanced by the state can, and there a big load can spread fixed costs and LOWER rates (per a Launch Alaska op-ed) or compete for scarce gas and RAISE them, decided by the rate deal. The gap: Anchorage adopted AO 2026-27 (10 to 2) while the statewide bill HB 259 sits in the House Energy Committee, so no statewide cost-allocation rule exists.
entries.headline: Will a data center raise your power bill?

entries.topic: A physics-informed machine-learning digital twin of one Utqiagvik permafrost road embankment (Penn State), built on two buried 1 km fiber-optic cables and three winters of thermal and seismic data (Sept 2021 to June 2024)
entries.angle: AI that reads Alaska instead of using it. The point is not a smarter guess, it is a forecast the ground itself keeps correcting; the power and the honest limit are the same fact (one lit kilometer). Net-new topic chosen to break a 4-deck data-center run; honesty-forward (single-source attributed, narrow ML not general AI, one prototype not a statewide deployment).
entries.headline: Reading the Frozen Ground at the Top of America
entries.outcome_notes: Score 8.81 (threshold 8.3), no hard fails. Chosen over an in-window STAK data-center re-touch (11 days after No.1, would be the 5th data-center deck) to protect feed variety. Single-source (Phys.org; JGR paywalled/not independently checked) framed honestly on S8 and in the first comment. Story ~5 weeks old, framed as recent research not breaking news.

entries.topic: Alaska schools writing the rules for AI in the classroom, anchored by Kenai Peninsula Borough School District's proposed AI policy
entries.angle: The tools arrived before the rules: KPBSD already spent $8,300 on a MagicSchool subscription while its governing AI policy is still a draft; the two real questions are who defines academic honesty and who guards student data, and the smart move is to write the policy in pencil (revisable) not ink. Framed against Alaska's 2025 DEED K-12 AI framework (32nd state, 7 principles, 6-step process) as labeled context.
entries.headline: AI is already in class. The rulebook is still in pencil.

entries.topic: AI acoustic and satellite monitoring of the endangered Cook Inlet beluga whale (NOAA Fisheries + Microsoft AI for Good + ADF&G dual-stage deep-learning detector-classifier)
entries.angle: A better ear is not a recovery. AI gives Alaska's most endangered whale a sharper always-on ear (and, separately, satellite eyes) that tells managers where and when the Cook Inlet belugas are in a wall of ship noise, but hearing is not healing: the machine measures the whale, it cannot move it. Net-new marine-mammal AI pivot off the fatigued data-center beat; honesty-forward (hedged 331, no invented accuracy figure).
entities: Alaska Department of Fish and Game
entries.headline: Now a machine can hear them through the ship noise.
entries.outcome_notes: Shipped 8.77 vs 8.3, 9 slides, vector PDF 7.8MB, zero hard fails. Full studio (6 scouts + fact-checker + 3 treatment-directors + copywriter + 5 pixel critics + flow-critic + scorer). Net-new marine-mammal acoustic AI, deliberate break from the 4-deck data-center fatigue. Weakest = artwork-craft (7): the S3 beluga shipped as the designed Canvas fallback (akthree transparent-subject snapshot sentinel false-negatived) and reads flat.
